settings.title: Fantasy Statblocks Settings
settings.language.name: Language
settings.language.desc: Set the language for the plugin. Defaults to Obsidian's language.
settings.general.heading: General Settings
settings.general.integrateDice.name: Integrate Dice Roller
settings.general.integrateDice.desc1: Add Dice Roller dice to statblocks by default. Use
settings.general.diceRollerNotInstalled: This setting is only usable with the Dice Roller plugin enabled.
settings.general.renderDice.name: Render Dice Rolls
settings.general.renderDice.desc1: Roll graphical dice inside statblocks by default. Use
settings.general.renderLinks.name: Try to Render Wikilinks
settings.general.renderLinks.desc1: The plugin will attempt to detect wikilinks inside Statblocks.
settings.general.renderLinks.desc2: Please note: these links will not be added to the graph.
settings.general.enableSRD.name: Enable 5e SRD
settings.general.enableSRD.desc: Use the Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition System Reference Document monsters.
settings.advanced.heading: Advanced Settings
settings.advanced.atomicWrite.name: Try to Save Data Atomically
settings.advanced.atomicWrite.desc1: This will cause the plugin to save data to a temporary file before saving the actual data file in an attempt to prevent data loss.
settings.advanced.atomicWrite.desc2: This can cause issues sometimes when using sync services.
settings.parse.heading: Note Parsing
settings.parse.autoParse.name: Automatically Parse Frontmatter for Creatures
settings.parse.autoParse.desc1: The plugin will watch the vault for creatures defined in note frontmatter.
settings.parse.autoParse.desc2: The "Parse Frontmatter for Creatures" command can also be used.
settings.parse.debug.name: Enable Debug Messages
settings.parse.debug.desc: Debug messages will be displayed by the file parser.
settings.parse.folder.name: Bestiary Folder
settings.parse.folder.desc: The plugin will only parse notes inside these folders and their children.
settings.layouts.heading: Layouts
settings.layouts.description1: New statblock layouts can be created and managed here. A specific layout can be used for a creature using the
settings.layouts.importJson.name: Import From JSON
settings.layouts.importJson.desc: Import a custom layout from a JSON file.
settings.layouts.defaultLayout.name: Default Layout
settings.layouts.defaultLayout.desc: Change the default statblock layout used, if not specified.
settings.layouts.showAdvanced.name: Show Advanced Options
settings.layouts.showAdvanced.desc: Show advanced options when editing layout blocks.
settings.layouts.error.noName: Invalid layout imported: layout does not have a name
settings.layouts.error.noBlocks: Invalid layout imported: no blocks defined in layout.
settings.layouts.error.importFailed: There was an error importing the layout:
settings.imports.heading: Import Homebrew Creatures
settings.imports.desc: Import creatures from creature files. Monsters are stored by name, so only the last creature by that name will be saved. This is destructive - any saved creature will be overwritten.
settings.imports.ownContent: Only import content that you own.
settings.imports.dndAppFile.name: Import DnDAppFile
settings.imports.improvedInitiative.name: Import Improved Initiative Data
settings.imports.critterDB.name: Import CritterDB Data
settings.imports.fiveETools.name: Import 5e.tools Data
settings.imports.tetraCube.name: Import TetraCube Data
settings.imports.pf2eMonsterTools.name: Import PF2eMonsterTools Data
settings.imports.pathbuilder.name: Import Pathbuilder Data
settings.imports.pathbuilder.desc: Import a PC or NPC exported from Pathbuilder2e.
settings.imports.generic.name: Import Generic Data
settings.imports.generic.desc1: Import generic JSON files. JSON objects will be imported
settings.imports.generic.desc2: and all objects must have the
settings.bestiary.heading: Bestiary
settings.error.displayTab: There was an error displaying the settings tab for 5e Statblocks.
modal.confirm.title: Are you sure?
modal.confirm.desc: This will delete {count} creatures. This cannot be undone.
modal.confirmImport.desc1: This Layout includes JavaScript blocks. JavaScript blocks can execute code in your vault, which could cause loss or corruption of data.
modal.confirmImport.desc2: Are you sure you want to import this layout?
